content: Current computer agents can be improved by reverse-engineering the dynamic interaction between the medial prefrontal cortex and hippocampus during memory retrieval, where the mPFC acts as a goal-directed controller that queries the hippocampus’s stored sensory and procedural memory traces to reconstruct contextually relevant action sequences for open-ended computer control tasks. This brain-inspired retrieval mechanism would let computer agents pull previously learned interactions with specific software interfaces adapt to new, unforeseen tasks without retraining, just as the human brain retrieves tailored memory to solve new problems.
